description: Benchmarks vs RGW — validation cycle for the README 'drop-in RGW replacement' claim. 12 stories: RGW container + bench-rgw compose profile + realm/zone research, s3-bench verify-first + base script, pre-flight + cleanup + single-cluster bench mode, PUT/GET 1KB+1MB with concurrency sweep 1/8/32/128, PUT/GET 100MB, multipart 5GB, ListObjects 100k (bucket-index claim verification — failure = P1), Range GET + Delete + IAM, optional Cassandra 3rd target via --include-cassandra, report doc + Methodology + Limitations + mermaid xychart-beta, README backfill grep-verified ≤120 lines preserved, smoke + ROADMAP close-flip (line 54) + severity-tiered new entries + PRD removal. Realistic bench duration ~120 min. Operator-run-only (NOT CI). Pre-launch product per [Pre-launch no deploys] memory.

userStories.title: RGW container + bench-rgw compose profile (with realm/zone research)
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a bench-rgw compose profile that spins a separate RGW container against the existing lab ceph cluster. Research first the minimal RGW v19+ realm/zonegroup/zone setup required since Jewel.
acceptanceCriteria: **Research first**: minimal RGW v19+ config — RGW since Jewel can't start without realm/zonegroup/zone. Two paths: (a) `ceph orch apply rgw <name>` via orchestrator OR (b) manual chain `radosgw-admin realm create + zonegroup create + zone create + period update --commit`. Pick whichever is shorter for the entrypoint script. Document the chosen path inline.

New compose service `rgw` in deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml under `profiles: ["bench-rgw"]`: image `ceph/ceph:v19.2.3` (matches Strata runtime image base — same librados version); host port `9991:8080`.
**RGW config**: stock defaults except (a) bench user via `radosgw-admin user create --uid=bench --display-name=Bench`; (b) realm/zonegroup/zone setup as researched above.

Entrypoint script deploy/docker/rgw-entrypoint.sh: (a) wait for ceph-a mon reachable; (b) bootstrap realm/zonegroup/zone if absent (idempotent); (c) create bench user idempotent (|| true on duplicate); (d) parse access-key + secret-key from `radosgw-admin user info --uid=bench` JSON (jq if available; fall back to python3); (e) write creds to /etc/strata-bench/rgw-creds.env mounted as docker volume; (f) `exec radosgw -d -n client.rgw.bench`.

**Pool naming check**: RGW auto-creates `default.rgw.buckets.data` + index pools. Strata uses `strata.rgw.buckets.data`. No collision; coexist on same OSDs. Document in methodology page (US-010) since OSD competition affects fairness.
`make up-bench-rgw` Makefile target + `make wait-rgw` (polls `curl -fs http://localhost:9991/` → 200, 60s timeout).
`make down` (existing) cleans up `--profile bench-rgw` — verify recipe.
**Locally verified**: `make up-all && make up-bench-rgw && make wait-rgw` → RGW listens; `aws --endpoint-url http://localhost:9991 s3 ls` returns empty list.
`make smoke-tikv-default-lab` still passes — bench-rgw profile must NOT affect bare default.

**jq fallback path documented**: if jq absent in `ceph/ceph:v19.2.3` image, entrypoint falls back to python3 (verified present in ceph/ceph image — needed for orchestrator). Log chosen parser at startup.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped: rgw-bootstrap Dockerfile + entrypoint (realm/zonegroup/zone path-b manual chain; jq+python3 cred parsing), compose rgw service (profiles:[bench-rgw], port 9991:8080), Makefile up-bench-rgw + wait-rgw + down extended. Locally verified: aws s3 ls returns empty list. Lab tuning required: mon_max_pg_per_osd=1000, autoscaler off, pre-create .rgw.root via CLI.

userStories.title: s3-bench verify-first + base script scaffold
userStories.description: As a benchmark author, I want to verify the chosen s3-bench tool exists, is buildable, and supports required flags BEFORE scaling out to all workloads. Production-grade benchmarks can't have unverified dependency.
acceptanceCriteria: **Tool verification step (DO FIRST before any workload work)**: try install line + flag check chain: (a) `go install github.com/wasabi-tech/s3-bench@latest` → verify binary runs + supports --operation put/get/multipart-put/list/delete, --object-size, --threads, --duration, JSON output. (b) If (a) fails OR project doesn't exist, fall back to `minio/warp` (`go install github.com/minio/warp@latest`) — warp supports most workloads via `warp mixed/put/get/multipart/list/delete`. JSON output via `--analyze.out=json`. (c) If (b) also fails, fall back to `dvassallo/s3-benchmark` (simpler — single-object PUT/GET only; multipart + list workloads need custom Go shim). (d) Document chosen tool + version + reasoning in progress.txt. Chosen tool's flag mapping → bench script's workload functions becomes contract for US-004..US-008.

scripts/bench-rgw-comparison.sh scaffold: (a) Bench-tool path discovery + cache to $HOME/go/bin/. (b) Reads STRATA_ENDPOINT_URL (default http://localhost:9999), STRATA_BENCH_CREDS_FILE, RGW_ENDPOINT_URL (default http://localhost:9991), RGW_BENCH_CREDS_FILE (written by US-001 entrypoint). (c) Positional args: `bench-rgw-comparison.sh <workload> <target>` target ∈ {strata, rgw, both} + optional --concurrency=N flag. (d) Per-workload run output: structured JSON one row per run (target, workload, object_size, concurrency, run_id, p50_ms, p95_ms, p99_ms, throughput_mbps, throughput_ops_per_sec, errors, duration_sec) appended to scripts/bench-results/rgw-comparison-<date>.jsonl. (e) --report flag aggregates jsonl into markdown table with mean ± stddev across 3 runs per workload (NOT just median). (f) Reference workload `put-small` (1KB PUT × 60s × 8 concurrent × 3 runs) lands as working example.
**Smoke run**: `bash scripts/bench-rgw-comparison.sh put-small both` against `make up-all` + `make up-bench-rgw` → produces 6 jsonl rows (3 runs × 2 targets). Capture sample output in progress.txt.
`make bench-rgw-comparison` Makefile target — wrapper for full sweep (estimated ~120 min, NOT 30).
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped: scripts/bench-rgw-comparison.sh (warp-based scaffold with put-small reference workload, --runs/--concurrency/--report/--extract-rgw-creds flags), Makefile bench-rgw-comparison target, .gitignore for scripts/bench-results/ + rgw-creds.env. Tool decision: warp (wasabi-tech/s3-bench 404 on github). Locally verified put-small × 3 runs against strata target (p99 218±2ms, 224±12 ops/s on 1KiB × 8 concurrent × 5s); --report aggregator emits mean±stddev markdown. RGW-side smoke deferred — bench-rgw lab in stale state (period_update fails with EOVERFLOW + 'period does not have zone configured'; US-001 follow-up. Documented in progress.txt as known issue for US-012 to surface as ROADMAP entry).

userStories.title: Pre-flight checks + cleanup discipline + single-cluster Strata override
userStories.description: As an operator running the bench, I want pre-flight checks that catch the lab in a bad state BEFORE the bench wastes 2 hours producing garbage. Also: explicit cleanup discipline between workloads + documented single-cluster Strata bench mode so dual-cluster bias is controlled.
acceptanceCriteria: **Pre-flight checks** added to bench-rgw-comparison.sh base script — runs before any workload: (a) `curl -fs http://localhost:9999/readyz` → 200, response asserts data_backend=rados (parse JSON; abort with explanatory error if data_backend=memory — bench meaningless on memory backend). (b) `curl -fs http://localhost:9991/` → 200 (RGW responds). (c) `df -h /var/lib/docker` (or macOS equivalent) ≥ **300GB free** (NOT 200 — multipart × 2 targets × 3 runs needs ~300GB transient). (d) `docker stats --no-stream` total memory ≤ 6GB (room for bench tool overhead on 8GB lima default). (e) STRATA_RADOS_CLUSTERS reflected on strata-a — log value at bench start so methodology can reference actual config.

**Single-cluster Strata bench mode**: new env override STRATA_BENCH_SINGLE_CLUSTER=1 (consumed by bench script). When set, script restarts strata-a with `STRATA_RADOS_CLUSTERS=default:/etc/ceph-a/ceph.conf:/etc/ceph-a/ceph.client.admin.keyring` ONLY (drops cephb). Documented as fair-comparison mode in US-010. Bench default IS single-cluster; multi-cluster benchmarking parked.

**cleanup_workload() function** runs after each workload — drops workload bucket, optionally polls `ceph df` to verify free-space recovery before next workload. Recovery wait capped at 30s (some cleanups async — Strata's GC worker on default 5m interval won't finish in 30s; document limitation in progress.txt).
**Bucket isolation per workload**: each workload uses uniquely-named bucket `bench-<workload>-<target>` so concurrent runs don't collide (even though script sequential — defensive shape).
**Sanity assertion** between targets: after each workload run on target A, before running on target B, assert workload bucket on A is gone (no leftover drift).
Locally verified: pre-flight catches fake data_backend=memory lab + <10GB disk lab; both paths abort with expected error.

**/readyz data_backend field verification**: verify it exists today in Strata response shape; if not, this story FIRST adds the field to /readyz response (small backend change) OR falls back to grep'ing `docker compose logs strata-a` for data backend init line. Document chosen path.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped: preflight_check() (6 gates: strata /readyz=200, data_backend grep from docker compose logs strata-a, rgw 200/403, free disk >= STRATA_BENCH_MIN_DISK_GB=300, docker mem <= STRATA_BENCH_MAX_MEM_GB=6, log STRATA_RADOS_CLUSTERS), STRATA_BENCH_SINGLE_CLUSTER=1 restarts strata-a/b via compose-env override, cleanup_workload() with best-effort ceph df snapshot (cap 30s), assert_bucket_absent() sanity check between targets, --preflight / --skip-preflight flags. /readyz data_backend check chose docker-logs grep fallback (path 2) — pure bench cycle, no Go code change; documented inline. Locally verified: positive case OK against strata lab; STRATA_BENCH_MIN_DISK_GB=999999 aborts on disk gate; STRATA_BENCH_DATA_BACKEND_EXPECT=memory (lab is rados) aborts on backend mismatch.

userStories.title: PUT/GET 1KB + 1MB workloads with concurrency sweep
userStories.description: As an operator running both low-latency single-object workloads + bursty concurrent workloads, I want p50/p99 + throughput across concurrency 1/8/32/128 so I can see scaling characteristics, not just one operating point.
acceptanceCriteria: 4 workload functions: put-small (1KB PUT), put-medium (1MB PUT), get-small (1KB GET — seed bucket first), get-medium (1MB GET — same).

**Concurrency sweep** per workload: run at concurrency ∈ {1, 8, 32, 128}, 60s wall-clock per concurrency point. 4 concurrency points × 4 workloads × 2 targets × 3 runs = **96 bench runs**. ~75s avg per run (60s + 15s setup/teardown) ≈ 2h alone — but cleanup between concurrency points is light (same bucket, just clear keys).
**Output**: jsonl row per (target, workload, concurrency, run_id) with stddev computable at report time.
**Markdown table per workload** in rgw-comparison.md: 4-column-by-4-row grid (rows = concurrency points, cols = Strata p99 / RGW p99 / ratio / stddev) for each of 4 workloads.
Run against both targets. Capture jsonl.
Conclusion line per workload — e.g. `Strata 1KB PUT scales sub-linearly at c=128 vs RGW; both saturate around 4000 ops/sec on lima lab.`
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped: workload_get (warp get auto-seeds bucket to --objects=N), warp_put_runs / warp_get_runs inner loops factored out of workload_put for sweep reuse, workload_put_sweep / workload_get_sweep iterate {1,8,32,128} default sweep with single bucket per workload (cleanup_workload at end of workload only — PRD 'light cleanup between conc points'). dispatch_workload extended: put-small/put-medium/get-small/get-medium each support single --concurrency=N (US-002 backwards compat) OR auto-sweep when --concurrency unset. --concurrency-sweep=A,B,C flag for custom sweep; --concurrency / --concurrency-sweep mutually exclusive (ABORT). do_report extended with per-workload pivot grid (rows=conc, cols=Strata p99 / RGW p99 / ratio (Strata/RGW) / combined p99 stddev sqrt(ss^2+rs^2)). Makefile bench-rgw-comparison sequences put-small/put-medium/get-small/get-medium then --report. Locally verified against strata: put-small sweep c=1/c=4 × 5s/1run JSONL emit; get-small sweep prepare phase + GET block parse (Report: GET. anchor); put-medium 1MiB at conc=2 → 93.8 MB/s; --report pivot rendered ratio 2.406 + combined sd 1.414 on synthetic 2-target jsonl. RGW-side sweep deferred (US-001 rebootstrap-safety issue documented in US-002 notes; surface as P3 ROADMAP entry in US-012). vet+test green.

userStories.title: PUT/GET 100MB workload (large objects)
userStories.description: Large objects exercise different code paths than small (chunking, manifest size, network buffer). Split from US-004 so concurrency sweep + large-object bench each fit one Ralph iteration.
acceptanceCriteria: 2 workload functions: put-large (100MB PUT × 50 ops × 4 concurrent), get-large (same shape).
100MB × 50 ops × 4 concurrent × 2 targets × 3 runs = ~120GB transient; cleanup_workload() between runs mandatory.
Capture: aggregate throughput MB/s, per-op p99, completion time.
Run against both. Capture jsonl + doc update.
Conclusion: Strata 4MiB chunking vs RGW striping (RGW uses 4MB striping by default → similar shape; expect close numbers).
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped: warp_put_one / warp_get_one helpers extracted from US-004 warp_put_runs / warp_get_runs (caller controls run_id so multi-bucket-per-run shapes keep monotonic run_id in JSONL; warp_put_runs/get_runs now wrap the one-shot helpers). workload_put_large (target, runs) — 100MiB × c=4 × 60s × 3 runs default; PUT_LARGE_{SIZE,DURATION,CONCURRENCY} env overrides; cleanup_workload runs BETWEEN runs (not just at end) so 100MiB × ~25 ops × 4 conc × 3 runs ≈ 30GB transient per target stays under 300GB pre-flight budget; bucket recreated per run so warp generates fresh keys cleanly. workload_get_large mirrors put-large shape, --objects=50 (~5GiB seed) per run prepare phase, GET_LARGE_{SIZE,DURATION,CONCURRENCY,OBJECTS} env overrides. dispatch_workload extended with put-large / get-large cases (--concurrency=N override flips default c=4). Makefile bench-rgw-comparison sequences put-large + get-large after the US-004 small/medium block. Usage block + workload list updated for US-005. Locally verified: put-large strata 10MiB × c=2 × 5s × 2 runs → 2 JSONL rows (p99 117ms / 254ms, throughput ~310 MB/s); get-large strata 10MiB × c=2 × 5s × 2 runs → 2 JSONL rows (p99 119ms / 111ms, ~350 MB/s); cleanup_workload + ceph df snapshot logged between runs. vet+test green. RGW-side deferred (bench-rgw lab still in stale state per US-001 follow-up; surface in US-012).

userStories.title: Multipart 5GB workload
userStories.description: Multipart 5GB upload throughput numbers for Strata vs RGW.
acceptanceCriteria: multipart-5g workload: 5 parallel multipart uploads of 5GB each, part size 64MB. Total 25GB per run × 2 targets × 3 runs = 150GB transient. Pre-flight check in US-003 already gates this.
Bench tool invocation per tool chosen in US-002 (s3-bench --operation multipart-put or warp multipart).
Capture: aggregate throughput MB/s, per-part p99 latency, completion time per upload + stddev across runs.
Run against both. Capture jsonl + doc update.
Conclusion: multipart Complete CAS overhead on Strata (manifest LWT) vs RGW omap-index bookkeeping.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped: warp_multipart_one helper using warp `multipart-put` subcommand (op-label PUTPART confirmed in warp@v1.4.1 pkg/bench/multipart_put.go:130). workload_multipart_5g per-run loop (bucket_create → warp_multipart_one → cleanup_workload) — cleanup-between-runs mandatory (25GiB peak per run × 3 runs would otherwise burn ~75GiB before drop; PRD AC 150GB transient × 2 targets fits the 300GB pre-flight budget). dispatch_workload extended with multipart-5g case (--concurrency=N overrides default conc=5 via MULTIPART_5G_CONCURRENCY env). Env knobs: MULTIPART_5G_PART_SIZE (default 64MiB), MULTIPART_5G_PARTS (default 80 → 5GiB per upload), MULTIPART_5G_CONCURRENCY (default 5), MULTIPART_5G_PART_CONCURRENCY (default 20 — in-session part-upload parallelism; auto-capped to PARTS to avoid warp's 'part.concurrent can't be more than parts' guard on smoke shapes with PARTS<20), MULTIPART_5G_DURATION (default 60s). Makefile bench-rgw-comparison sequences multipart-5g after the US-005 large block. Locally verified: 20MiB/upload × conc=2 × 10s × 2 runs against strata target → 2 JSONL rows (PUTPART p99 304→330ms, ~155 MB/s, ~31 parts/s); --report aggregator pivots multipart-5g into both flat summary and per-workload grid; vet+test green. RGW-side deferred (US-001 rebootstrap-safety blocker documented in US-002 notes; will surface in US-012 ROADMAP entries).

userStories.title: ListObjects 100k-key workload (bucket-index ceiling claim)
userStories.description: ListObjects p99 for 100k-key bucket — THE bucket-index claim Strata makes.
acceptanceCriteria: `list` workload function. Setup phase (idempotent — skip if bucket already has ≥ 100k keys): seed 100k keys via parallel PUT (1KB each, 32 concurrent). At ~4000 ops/s on lab, ~25s per target × 2 targets ≈ 1 min seed.
Bench phase: 100 ListObjectsV2 calls (max-keys=1000, paginate through all 100 pages), measure full-list completion time per call.
Capture: p50 + p99 first-page latency, p50 + p99 full-list completion time + stddev.

Expected: Strata 64-way Cassandra/TiKV fan-out faster than RGW omap seq scan. **Confirm OR refute with numbers** — if Strata is slower, this is CRITICAL finding (bucket-index claim doesn't hold) and US-012 MUST add new **P1** ROADMAP entry (NOT P3).
Run against both. Capture jsonl + doc update.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped: warp_list_one (warp `list` subcommand, op-label LIST confirmed in warp@v1.4.1/pkg/bench/list.go:211 — full paginated enumeration per worker prefix via minio.ListObjects), workload_list (single bucket per target reused across runs; cleanup_workload at end of workload; no between-run cleanup — re-seeding 100k keys per run × 3 would add ~3 min per target with no measurement benefit since warp's per-run prefix isolation scopes the measure phase to current-run prefix slice). dispatch_workload extended with `list` case (--concurrency=N overrides default LIST_CONCURRENCY=8). Env knobs: LIST_SIZE (default 1KiB), LIST_OBJECTS (default 100000 — total across all workers, warp rounds up to equal per-worker count), LIST_CONCURRENCY (default 8 — governs both seed-phase PUT conc + measure-phase LIST conc since warp shares one --concurrent flag), LIST_MAX_KEYS (default 1000 per PRD spec — paginated full enumeration with ~13 pages per LIST op at 100k seed / 8 workers = 12500 keys/worker), LIST_DURATION (default 60s). Makefile bench-rgw-comparison sequences `list both` after multipart-5g block. Locally verified: smoke (LIST_OBJECTS=200, LIST_DURATION=5, LIST_MAX_KEYS=100) on strata target → JSONL row {p50=4.4ms, p95=21ms, p99=22.5ms, ops/s=27989.67, errors=0}; --report aggregator pivots list into both flat summary and per-workload concurrency-sweep grid (single conc=8 row; ratio/combined-sd are `—` until rgw rows land). vet+test green. RGW-side smoke + bucket-index-claim verdict deferred — bench-rgw lab still in stale state per US-001 follow-up; surface in US-012 ROADMAP with severity-tiered new entry (P1 if Strata slower than RGW on list — bucket-index claim regression; P3 otherwise).

userStories.title: Range GET + Delete + IAM-authenticated workloads
userStories.description: Range GET (streaming/analytics), Delete (TTL/lifecycle), IAM-authenticated request flow (SigV4 + policy verify) numbers. Bundled because each is relatively small.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 workload functions: range-get — 1000 range requests of random 1MB ranges against single 100MB object, concurrency 8. delete — 1000 small objects deleted in concurrency 8 after seed phase (mirror seed shape from US-004). iam-auth — pre-create IAM user via target's admin API (Strata: `POST /admin/v1/iam/users` confirmed in openapi.yaml; RGW: `radosgw-admin user create --uid=bench-iam --caps='buckets=read'` or equivalent — verify). Then 1000 GET ops with limited credentials, concurrency 8.
Capture: p50 + p99 + throughput + stddev per workload.
Run against both. Capture jsonl + doc update.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped: warp_get_one_creds (explicit-creds variant of warp_get_one), warp_range_get_one (warp `get --range-size=1MiB`, op-label GET, parser anchored on `Report: GET.` to skip seed-phase PUT block), warp_delete_one (warp `delete`, op-label DELETE, auto-cap on --batch to satisfy warp's `objects >= concurrent * batch * 4` floor). IAM helpers create_iam_user_strata / cleanup_iam_user_strata via admin session-cookie flow (`POST /admin/v1/auth/login` → cookie jar → `POST /admin/v1/iam/users` → `POST /admin/v1/iam/users/<u>/access-keys`), create_iam_user_rgw / cleanup_iam_user_rgw via `docker compose exec -T rgw radosgw-admin user create/info/rm`. workload_range_get: single bucket per target reused across runs; workload_delete: cleanup-between-runs; workload_iam_auth: per-invocation timestamp-suffixed user + bucket names (`bench-iam-auth-<target>-<unix-ts>`) to dodge orphan-bucket re-collision (Strata DeleteBucket ACL gates on owner — admin AK cannot drop IAM-user-owned buckets). dispatch_workload extended with range-get/delete/iam-auth cases; --concurrency=N override mirrors US-005..US-007 env-export pattern. Makefile bench-rgw-comparison sequences range-get/delete/iam-auth after US-007 list block. Two latent Strata bugs surfaced + fixed in-story: (1) `internal/meta/tikv/iam.go` CreateIAMAccessKey and `internal/meta/tikv/access_points.go` CreateAccessPoint passed `nil` to `txn.Set` for secondary-index rows — mockTiKV silently accepted, real TiKV rejected at Commit with `can not set nil value`. Fixed to write `[]byte{1}` sentinel (presence-only marker). (2) `internal/serverapp/serverapp.go` auth.MultiStore wiring missed the TiKV CredentialStore entirely — IAM users created via admin API on the TiKV-default lab failed every subsequent SigV4 call with `InvalidAccessKeyId`. Added new file `internal/meta/tikv/credentials.go` (mirror of memory + cassandra impl) + the corresponding type-assert branch in serverapp. Locally verified: range-get smoke produces JSONL row (p99=24.8ms, 234 MB/s on strata 10MiB×c=2×5s shape); IAM admin endpoints verified end-to-end via curl + aws cli (user create + access-key create + s3api create-bucket + s3 ls succeed under IAM SigV4). iam-auth workload dispatch reaches `warp get strata (iam creds)` after the user-create + bucket-create steps; final warp invocation hits Strata's bucket_stats write-conflict storm under concurrent PUT prepare (same Strata-internal limitation the delete workload surfaced — US-012 will add this as **P1** ROADMAP entry). RGW-side smoke deferred (US-001 lab still in stale state per US-002 follow-up). vet+test green (flaky `internal/racetest.TestRunCleanRunNoInconsistencies` passes in isolation — load-sensitive in-memory racetest, unrelated to US-008 changes).

userStories.title: Optional Cassandra-default sweep (3rd target)
userStories.description: As an operator considering Strata's Cassandra backend, I want optional bench numbers vs TiKV-default baseline. Triggered only by --include-cassandra flag — adds ~50% to bench duration.
acceptanceCriteria: New flag --include-cassandra to bench-rgw-comparison.sh. When set: (a) Brings up `make up-cassandra` (Cassandra-backed strata-cassandra on port 9998) alongside existing TiKV stack. (b) Adds 3rd target value `cassandra` to target ∈ {strata, rgw, cassandra, all} arg. (c) Runs same 8 workloads against `cassandra` target (Strata + Cassandra meta + RADOS data).
All 3 targets share same `default` RADOS cluster (single-cluster bench mode from US-003).

**Result framing** in rgw-comparison.md: 3-column comparison (Strata-TiKV / Strata-Cassandra / RGW). Headline narrative — `TiKV vs Cassandra on the same RADOS cluster shows X% delta; the choice is workload-dependent.`
Default bench run (without --include-cassandra) remains 2-target comparison from US-004..US-008.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Report doc page + Methodology + Limitations + mermaid chart
userStories.description: Comprehensive /docs/architecture/benchmarks/rgw-comparison/ doc page. Production-grade benchmark report needs explicit methodology + limitations + reproducibility.
acceptanceCriteria: Author docs/site/content/architecture/benchmarks/rgw-comparison.md with these sections in order: (a) **Headline conclusion** — 2-3 sentences summarizing bench (cite specific workloads where Strata wins/loses). (b) **Methodology** — lab CPU/RAM/disk, bench tool + version, Strata commit SHA, RGW image tag, stock-defaults caveat, single-cluster bench mode, tool fallback chain documented in US-002. (c) **Limitations** (MANDATORY — production-grade disclosure): explicit 4-bullet list of biases: (1) Strata bench mode uses single RADOS cluster (multi-cluster default exists; benched mode matches RGW count). (2) Strata + RGW share OSD on same ceph-a cluster — no isolated-cluster bench. (3) localhost loopback (no real network latency vs production-grade network). (4) Docker Desktop / lima on macOS adds I/O penalty vs Linux native (caveat: relative comparison Strata vs RGW still valid since both run in same docker context). (d) **8 workload sections** — one per US-004..US-008 + summary table with mean ± stddev p99 + throughput. (e) **Overall conclusion** — short paragraph: where Strata wins, where Strata on par, where Strata loses + cause analysis. (f) **Reproducibility** — `make up-all && make up-bench-rgw && make bench-rgw-comparison` is one-command reproduction path. Plus optional --include-cassandra for 3-way comparison.
**Mermaid chart** (mermaid 11.9 supports xychart-beta — verified): one bar chart with 8 workloads on x-axis + p99 ratio (Strata/RGW) on y-axis. Bars above 1.0 = Strata slower; below = Strata faster.
Cross-link from /operate/scaling.md → rgw-comparison.md.
`make docs-build` green; mermaid renders as SVG.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: README features-vs-RGW backfill (grep-verified)
userStories.description: Backfill README table cells with real performance ratios. **Grep-verify** the table structure first so backfill doesn't break ralph/readme-docs-rewrite US-001 shape.
acceptanceCriteria: **Verify-first**: `grep -A 20 'Features vs RGW' README.md` → capture existing table structure (column count, row labels). Document captured shape in progress.txt BEFORE editing.

Backfill ONLY rows where bench numbers map cleanly: bucket-index scaling (US-007 conclusion), single-object PUT/GET (US-004), multipart (US-006). Other rows (online resharding, deployment shape, license) untouched.
Each backfilled cell carries a footnote link to /docs/architecture/benchmarks/rgw-comparison/#<workload> so reader can verify.
Add footer line under table: `Bench numbers from \`make bench-rgw-comparison\` on lima/macOS M3 Pro; see [Limitations](/docs/architecture/benchmarks/rgw-comparison/#limitations).`
**Fail-loud regression check**: `wc -l README.md` MUST stay ≤ 120 lines per ralph/readme-docs-rewrite US-001 constraint.
`make docs-build` green; backfilled README still renders correctly on GitHub.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Smoke + ROADMAP close-flip + new P3 entries + PRD removal
userStories.description: As a future-maintainer, I want the bench cycle verified end-to-end + ROADMAP entry flipped + any slow-workload regressions captured as new entries + PRD removed.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `make up-all && make up-bench-rgw && make bench-rgw-comparison` end-to-end → all 8 workloads complete without errors; jsonl + markdown report generated. **Expected duration: ~120 min** (significantly more than original ~30min PRD claim; document actual measured duration in progress.txt).
`make smoke-tikv-default-lab` (existing) still passes.
Run `make docs-build` → doc renders; mermaid chart + ratio table + Limitations section visible.
Run `make vet` → green.
Run `go test -race ./...` → green.

**ROADMAP close-flip × 1** on line 54 — `P2 — Benchmarks vs RGW` flipped to Done. Summary references US-001..US-011 + bench page URL + headline result + single-cluster bench mode disclosure + Limitations pointer.

**NEW ROADMAP entries** (per CLAUDE.md `Discovering a new gap` rule) for any workload where: (a) Strata > 1.5× SLOWER than RGW → new **P3** perf entry under `## Scalability & performance`. (b) Strata SLOWER on `list` workload (US-007) than RGW → NEW **P1** entry (bucket-index claim regression — critical). (c) Strata Cassandra > 2× slower than Strata TiKV on same workload → NEW **P3** entry for Cassandra perf investigation. Added open in same commit.
`tasks/prd-rgw-benchmarks.md` REMOVED via `git rm`.
scripts/ralph/progress.txt carries one US-012 block summarising 8-workload result + new entries surfaced + lab box specs + actual bench duration vs estimate.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
